a.xlink:title: Main driver routine for the HELIOS SIE simulation program.
Set number of periodic evaluation terms (for periodic GFs).
Configure how many threads to use for parallel assembly.
Set thread count used internally by SimJob.
Close the currently open file.
Convert/modify a mesh according to a conversion job file.
Enable globally more accurate (and usually slower) evaluation.
Request high‑accuracy evaluation paths (global).
Enable high-accuracy integration for excitations.
Evaluate fields on a set of points read from a file.
Return the current open tag (stack top), or empty string if none.
Load binary lookup table used by periodic Green's function acceleration.
Open a job file.
Read the next tag token and update the open-tag stack.
High-level entry: run a simulation for a given job file.
Load a mesh from file by extension.
Remove all triangles on or below a plane (strict > 1e-10 kept).
Write current mesh to the custom ".mesh" format.
Replace all occurrences of domain ID n1 with n2 in triangles.
Read mesh from the custom ".mesh" text format.
Read mesh from a COMSOL ".mphtxt" file.
Read mesh from a Gmsh ".msh" file (v2 text).
Read a value of type T from a text stream, skipping lines beginning with '#'.
Return index of a node pointer within the owned node array.
Absorption cross-section.
Close and deallocate a set of open output files.
Delete and clear all incident sources.
Compute the element-wise complex conjugate of a 3-vector.
Find which domain contains a point.
Allocate and zero a new solution vector x, and append to solVectors.
Extinction cross-section.
Thread worker to evaluate fields over posvec in parallel.
Check whether any incident field is a dipole.
Parse a full block from the already-open JobParser.
Load a previously saved solution vector into solVectors.back().
Parse one (incident specification + label).
Net time-averaged power crossing the domain's surface.
Extract the real part of each component of a complex 3-vector.

Absorption cross section.

Extinction cross section.
Return both , incident and secondary pairs at pos.
Skip whitespace (ws); position on next non-ws.
Allocate and zero the system matrix A (and gradients if enabled).
